article_meta.title: PoliticsMeet the New Proud BoysThe far-right group's views and tactics are now emulated by federal agents.By Ali BrelandIllustration by The Atlantic

reasoning: The model correctly avoided quoted/attributed language and flagged several genuine instances of author-level bias with mostly appropriate labels. However, it missed multiple prominent biased framings in the author's prose and applied one debatable label (Slant) where Opinion-as-Fact/Word Choice would fit better.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the author's own narration; no quotes or extended reproduced statements were treated as author bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged item reflects clear authorial framing or loaded language; none are neutral or misattributed.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed several strong author-level claims: e.g., 'opened the way for extremist militias to antagonize local populations,' 'groups of armed men ... intimidating immigrants,' 'of course' (editorializing), 'relentless intimidation' and 'militaristic tactics,' 'Nativist ideologies are driving immigration policy,' and 'DHS is disseminating white-supremacist propaganda.'
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Most labels fit; however, labeling the DOJ sentence as Slant is inaccurate—it's more aptly Opinion Statements Presented as Fact and/or Word Choice/Spin.
